evidence: SketchHandlers.handleSurfaceRelations and handleDepRelations are single-line pass-through wrappers: each does nothing but forward (exchange, RelationType.X) to handleRelationsForType — no auth, logging, or transformation added

GrammarConfigLoader.createDefaultEnglish() duplicates the exact try/catch/IllegalStateException pattern from createDefaultEnglish(String configPath) instead of delegating to it; zero-arg overload could be a one-liner calling the string overload

VisualizationHandlers.handleVisualRadial logs the full request body at debug level: logger.debug("Radial: body = {}", body) — body can be several kilobytes of JSON; this is a nosy object-dump pattern, even if benign at debug level

GrammarConfigLoader.fromReader Javadoc includes a trivial usage example (try (Reader r = new StringReader(jsonContent)) { GrammarConfigLoader.fromReader(r); }) that restates the method signature without adding information — the method purpose is self-evident from the signature

strengths: Javadoc comments throughout explain design decisions and WHY, not just WHAT — e.g. HttpApiUtils class-level Javadoc explains why response and parameter concerns coexist in one class rather than restating what the methods do
Method names are consistently domain-specific: executeDependencyPattern, exploreByPattern, resolveRelationAlias, deriveTokenPosition — no generic handleData or processItem placeholders in domain code
Error handling is centralised in wrapWithErrorHandling rather than copy-pasted into every route handler — the codebase resists the boilerplate-duplication pattern
No entry/exit logging on methods; logging is present only where state changes or warnings are meaningful (e.g. skipping a malformed relation, CORS wildcard warning)
SemanticFieldExplorer class Javadoc provides a worked algorithm example with concrete output — this is domain explanation, not code restatement
ai_generated_debt.issue_character: Issues are isolated and structural rather than pervasive: a pair of trivially thin pass-through wrappers, one case of duplicated exception handling that could delegate, and one nosy debug log dumping a full request body — the pattern is 'AI-generated convenience skeletons left unfactored' rather than systemic commentary bloat or defensive overengineering.
ai_generated_debt.score_rationale: The codebase mostly avoids the canonical AI-debt hallmarks: comments explain reasoning rather than restating code, names are domain-anchored, and there is no defensive null-checking of typed values or copy-pasted error handling across the whole codebase. The deductions come from three localised issues: two literal one-line pass-through wrappers (handleSurfaceRelations/handleDepRelations), one case of duplicated try/catch boilerplate between the two createDefaultEnglish overloads that should have been a delegation, and one full-body debug dump. These are real but narrow problems confined to a single handler class and one loader method. The overall quality sits solidly in the 'solid but uneven' band — strong in most places, with a handful of unfactored conveniences that match typical LLM-generated skeleton patterns.

issues.summary: handleSurfaceRelations and handleDepRelations are pure pass-through wrappers with no added logic
evidence: handleSurfaceRelations(HttpExchange) is a single-line method that calls handleRelationsForType(exchange, RelationType.SURFACE) with no other logic
handleDepRelations(HttpExchange) is a single-line method that calls handleRelationsForType(exchange, RelationType.DEP) with no other logic
Neither wrapper adds auth, logging, transformation, or any contract beyond forwarding arguments — they exist solely as named aliases for the type constant
issues.suggestion: Eliminate the wrappers: in WordSketchApiServer route registration, pass lambdas directly — e.g. exchange -> sketchHandlers.handleRelationsForType(exchange, RelationType.SURFACE) — or make handleRelationsForType package-accessible and route to it directly. If caller-visible stable method names are needed for testability, document that explicitly.

issues.summary: createDefaultEnglish() duplicates the try/catch/IllegalStateException pattern from createDefaultEnglish(String) instead of delegating
evidence: createDefaultEnglish(String configPath) at line 223 has a try block catching FileNotFoundException and IOException, wrapping both in IllegalStateException

createDefaultEnglish() at line 252 contains an identical try/catch structure with the same exception types and the same wrapping strategy, differing only in appending 'Set -Dgrammar.config=<path> to override.' to the messages
The zero-arg overload resolves a system property then could simply call createDefaultEnglish(resolvedPath) with the suffix appended in the message — duplicating the entire catch block is unnecessary
issues.suggestion: Refactor the zero-arg overload to delegate: resolve the system property, then call createDefaultEnglish(path), catching IllegalStateException to append the -Dgrammar.config override hint. Or add a private helper that performs the load-and-wrap with a caller-supplied hint string, eliminating the duplication.

issues.summary: VisualizationHandlers dumps the full request body at debug level on every radial request
evidence: Line 55: logger.debug("Radial: body = {}", body) — body is the complete JSON request body, potentially several kilobytes
Line 64: logger.debug("Radial: center = {}", center) — a second debug log immediately after for a single extracted field, redundant given the body is already logged
No other HTTP handlers in the package log the full request body; this is an isolated remnant of exploratory debugging
issues.suggestion: Remove both debug log statements. If body-level tracing is needed during development, replace with a size-bounded summary: logger.debug("Radial: body size={} bytes, center={}", body.length(), center) to avoid dumping arbitrary user data into logs.

root_causes: Convenience skeleton generation: pass-through wrappers and duplicated try/catch blocks are typical artifacts of LLM-generated scaffolding that was not subsequently refactored into proper delegation
Debug instrumentation left in place: the radial body dump reads like exploratory printf-debugging that was never removed after the endpoint was verified working
